title: Per-Origin Politeness, Token Timing, Retry Budgets, and Fairness · Language Lens: Origin Fairness
summary: A shared ticker spaces starts globally, one origin monopolizes slots, Retry-After is ignored, jitter is unbounded, and transient failures retry forever. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go origin fairness increment in monotonic per-origin eligibility scheduler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Select among eligible origins with starvation bounds, stable priorities, owner commitments, and visible queue-age evidence.
Parse valid Retry-After and combine with capped exponential backoff and bounded jitter while recording the chosen schedule.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
